files.path: Gemini CLI Subagents  High-Leverage Workflow Playbook.md
files.reason: File is already a well-structured playbook on Gemini CLI subagents. All three parts (mechanics, 5 highest-leverage moves, frontier) carry tier-1/2 content with concrete YAML examples, named tools, version-specific parameters, and OSS project references. Compressed by ~35% (12.8 KB → 8 KB) but full coverage retained.

Adjacent product (Gemini Deep Research API, not CLI subagents). Every section is tier-1 specifics — exact parameter names (background=True, visualization='auto', collaborative_planning, previous_interaction_id, thinking_summaries='auto'), time-budgeted actions (today / next week / frontier), and uncommon strategies (hybrid MCP search, recursive chains, batch + meta-synthesis, dynamic tool gating).

files.path: _archive/Here is Gemini CLI’s March 18th weekly update for.md
files.reason: Versioned release notes (v0.34.0). Release notes go stale fast; durable items (JIT GEMINI.md loading, /chat→/resume rename, mcpName standardisation) are folded into the main playbook's architecture sections. Considered partial-keep but the file's value-density per KB is low for a long-lived knowledge base.

files.path: _archive/I came accross this post . Please can you extract.md
files.reason: Compressed/condensed version of the High-Leverage Workflow Playbook file. ~80% overlap. Every framing, example, and recommendation is more thoroughly stated in the source. Pure duplication.

Surface-level Gemini Embedding 2 use-case list (RAG, semantic search, reranking, sentiment, clustering). Textbook embedding 101. For a user with marketing/PPC expertise and an established AI workflow, no tier-1/2/3 content.
